title: carex salina subsp. subspathacea
intro: Carex salina subsp. subspathacea, also known as Salt-sedge, is a subspecies of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and infections. The health benefits of Carex salina subsp. subspathacea include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and even exhibit antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various skin conditions and infections.
